what_it_does: This file is a posted noticeboard note in which Tim records a short point agreeing with a prior observation about over-capture, contrasting the minimal human-facing card with the full machine-facing envelope.
observations: YAML frontmatter declares id, address, type=note, source=claude_code, state=posted, title 'Tim · point', author_session=tim, channel=dragnet-development, and a commented_on link to item-7eb16874.
Created and updated timestamps are identical, with a single history entry showing filing.
Body uses a [point] tag and quotes/paraphrases a prior remark about Tim's MSG2 complaint that the composer echoes selected content back to the user.
It endorses the 'same envelope, two renderings' resolution: minimal/elided context for the human view, full structured context for the agent.
standouts: Agreement with the over-capture critique and the human-vs-machine rendering split.
The principle 'Do not conflate what Tim sees with what the member receives.'
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item YAML schema with id, address, type, source, state, author_session, channel, links, and history.
Uses a markdown body with a [point] tag to label the note's function.
References another board item via the links.commented_on field.
notes: A brief design-note endorsement of keeping human UI minimal while preserving full structured context for the agent.
questions: What is MSG2 and where is it recorded?
What is the full content of the referenced item-7eb16874?
What specific composer or UI component is being discussed?
Who or what is meant by 'the member' in this context?
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to capture and ratify a design insight: the same underlying envelope should render differently for a human reader and an agent, avoiding redundant on-screen echo.
contribution: Records a concise design rationale that human-visible context can be elided while the agent envelope retains full structure, useful for future UI/agent envelope decisions.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard note by Tim endorsing the split between minimal human-facing card rendering and full structured machine-facing envelope rendering to avoid over-capture.
intention_for_embedding: To record agreement with a design principle that separates on-screen human context from the structured context sent to an agent.
